title: Heavy library loaded eagerly
message: "recharts" ships extra code to your users up front & slows page load. Load it on demand with React.lazy() or next/dynamic.
help: Load it only when needed: `const Component = dynamic(() => import('library'), { ssr: false })` from next/dynamic, or React.lazy().

title: Missing effect dependencies
message: `useEffect` can run with a stale `setupCanvas` & show your users old data.

title: Large component is hard to read and change
message: Component "RetroGrid" is 312 lines long, which is hard to read & change. Split it into a few smaller components.
help: Pull each section into its own component so the parent is easier to read, test, and change.

title: State initialized from a mount effect
message: Your users see an extra render with empty "isWebGlReady" because a useEffect sets its starting value.
help: Pass the initial value directly to useState() instead of setting it from a mount-only useEffect. For SSR hydration, prefer useSyncExternalStore().

title: Pure function rebuilt every render
message: `normalizeUrl` inside `SocialMediaEditor` uses no local state but is rebuilt on every render, so it wastes work & breaks memoized children. Move it to the top of the file, outside the component.
help: Move the function above the component, at the top of the file. It doesn't use local state, so rebuilding it each update is wasted work.

title: Prop derived into useState
message: Your users see a stale value when prop "profile" changes because useState copies it once.
help: Compute the value inline so prop changes do not leave `useState` holding a stale copy.

title: Prop derived into useState
message: Your users see a stale value when prop "profile" changes because useState copies it once.
help: Compute the value inline so prop changes do not leave `useState` holding a stale copy.

title: Role used instead of HTML tag
message: Screen reader users get more reliable semantics from `<input>` than `role="slider"`, so use `<input>` instead.
help: Use the matching HTML element when one exists so browsers and assistive tech get native semantics.

title: Role used instead of HTML tag
message: Screen reader users get more reliable semantics from `<input>` than `role="slider"`, so use `<input>` instead.
help: Use the matching HTML element when one exists so browsers and assistive tech get native semantics.

title: Overly precise SVG path values
message: Your users download extra bytes for SVG d precision they can't see, so round it to 1 or 2 decimals.
help: Round path, points, and transform decimals to 1 or 2 digits. The extra precision adds bytes with no visible difference.

title: Pure function rebuilt every render
message: `scrollToFeatures` inside `Header` uses no local state but is rebuilt on every render, so it wastes work & breaks memoized children. Move it to the top of the file, outside the component.
help: Move the function above the component, at the top of the file. It doesn't use local state, so rebuilding it each update is wasted work.

title: Plain img ships unoptimized images
message: Plain ships unoptimized, oversized images.
help: Use `next/image` so users get optimized formats, responsive srcsets, and lazy loading instead of oversized image downloads.

title: Large inline style object rebuilds every render
message: This inline style has 11 properties, which is hard to read & rebuilds every render. Move it to a CSS class, CSS module, or styled component.
help: Move the styles to a CSS class, CSS module, Tailwind utilities, or a styled component. Big inline objects are hard to read and rebuild on every update.

title: Large inline style object rebuilds every render
message: This inline style has 10 properties, which is hard to read & rebuilds every render. Move it to a CSS class, CSS module, or styled component.
help: Move the styles to a CSS class, CSS module, Tailwind utilities, or a styled component. Big inline objects are hard to read and rebuild on every update.

title: Multiple components in one file
message: This file declares several components, so each component is harder to find, test, and change.
help: Move secondary components into their own files so each component stays easier to find, test, and change.

title: Multiple components in one file
message: This file declares several components, so each component is harder to find, test, and change.
help: Move secondary components into their own files so each component stays easier to find, test, and change.

help: Delete the file if it is truly unreachable, or import it from an entry point.

title: React 19 API migration can break callers
message: forwardRef is dead weight in React 19, since ref is a normal prop now, so drop it & pass ref straight through.
help: Pass `ref` as a normal prop on function components, since `forwardRef` isn't needed in React 19. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)`. Only runs on React 19+ projects.

title: Redundant manual memoization
message: This `useCallback` is dead weight, since React Compiler already caches every function here. Delete it.
help: Delete the `useMemo` / `useCallback` / `memo` call and use the plain value or component. React Compiler caches it for you.

title: React 19 API migration can break callers
message: forwardRef is dead weight in React 19, since ref is a normal prop now, so drop it & pass ref straight through.
help: Pass `ref` as a normal prop on function components, since `forwardRef` isn't needed in React 19. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)`. Only runs on React 19+ projects.

title: Label missing associated control
message: Screen reader users can't tell which input this label names because it's tied to none, so add `htmlFor` or wrap the input inside it.
help: Tie every label to a control with `htmlFor`, or by nesting the input.

title: React 19 API migration can break callers
message: useContext is replaced by `use()` in React 19, which reads context inside ifs & loops too, so switch to `import { use } from 'react'`.

title: Derived value copied into state
message: Storing "internalValue" in state when you can derive it from other values costs an extra render.
help: Work out the value while rendering (or with useMemo if it's expensive) instead of copying it into useState through a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#updating-state-based-on-props-or-state

title: Zod 3 schema API breaks in Zod 4
message: This Zod 3 schema API changed in Zod 4, so this schema can fail after the upgrade.
help: Switch to the Zod 4 versions: top-level factories like `z.enum()`, object helpers like `z.strictObject()`, the new `z.function({ input, output })` form, and explicit key/value schemas for `z.record()`.

message: Unused export: `EMPTY_STYLE` is exported but no module imports it, so it expands the public surface and can mislead callers about supported API.
help: Drop the `export` keyword (or remove the declaration) if no other module uses this symbol.

message: pnpm-workspace.yaml is missing `minimumReleaseAge` — newly published versions can ship malware that gets caught and unpublished within hours
help: Add `minimumReleaseAge: 10080` (7 days) to pnpm-workspace.yaml to delay installs until releases have had time to be vetted

message: pnpm-workspace.yaml is missing `trustPolicy` — without `no-downgrade`, pnpm silently accepts packages whose trust signals (provenance, signatures) weaken between updates
help: Add `trustPolicy: no-downgrade` to pnpm-workspace.yaml

message: Unused export: `deleteProfile` is exported but no module imports it, so it expands the public surface and can mislead callers about supported API.
help: Drop the `export` keyword (or remove the declaration) if no other module uses this symbol.

title: await inside a loop
message: This makes the for…of loop slow because each await runs one after another, so collect the independent calls & run them together with `await Promise.all(items.map(...))`
help: Collect the items, then use `await Promise.all(items.map(...))` so independent work runs at the same time

title: Format method on z.string()
message: This `z.string().<format>()` check is deprecated in Zod 4, so it can break during the upgrade.
help: Use the Zod 4 top-level format checks like `z.email()`, `z.uuid()`, or `z.ipv4()` instead of `z.string().<format>()`.
